intro.title: The March on Rome
intro.body: Hannibal Barca led ~50,000 men and 37 elephants from New Carthage across the Alps in autumn 218 BCE. Three battles in three years — Trebia, Trasimene, Cannae — annihilated Roman field armies but did not break the Republic.

stops.name: New Carthage
stops.note: Carthaginian Iberian capital; departure point May 218 BCE (Polybius III.35). Note: outside Eastern Mediterranean region pack — shown for context.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Iberian harbour city under twin headlands, elephants embarking on transports, sun rising east.

stops.name: Rhône crossing
stops.note: Hannibal rafts his elephants across the Rhône near modern Avignon, eluding a Roman force at the river mouth (Polybius III.46).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Wide river, elephants on rafts of bound timber, Gallic skirmishers on the far bank.

stops.name: Alps crossing
stops.note: Fifteen-day passage, October 218 BCE. Snow, rockslide, and elephant deaths; Polybius III.50-56 is the primary account. The exact pass is debated.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Mountain pass in early snow, column of elephants and infantry on a narrow track, ravine below.

stops.name: Lake Trasimene
stops.note: 21 June 217 BCE. Hannibal ambushes the consul Flaminius in fog along the lake shore; ~15,000 Romans killed (Polybius III.83-84).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Misty lakeshore, Roman column strung along narrow road, Carthaginian infantry descending from wooded hills.

stops.name: Cannae
stops.note: 2 August 216 BCE. Hannibal's double-envelopment destroys eight Roman legions on the Apulian plain (Polybius III.107-117).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Apulian plain, crescent-shaped Carthaginian line absorbing Roman center, cavalry closing both flanks.
